name: Fudgey Pecan Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Line the bottom of a 9-inch springform pan with aluminum foil and butter the foil and side of the pan. Set aside.
3. In a large mixing bowl, combine melted butter, sugar, and vanilla extract. Beat well until smooth.
4. Add egg yolks one at a time to the mixture, beating well after each addition. Blend in cocoa powder, flour, vegetable oil, and water; beat until thoroughly combined. Stir in chopped pecans.
5. In a separate small mixing bowl, beat egg whites, cream of tartar, and salt with an electric mixer until stiff peaks form.
6. Carefully fold the egg white mixture into the chocolate batter using a spatula until just combined.
7. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and smooth the top with a spatula.
8. Bake for 45 minutes or until the center begins to crack slightly (do not test for doneness in the center). Allow to cool on a wire rack for 1 hour.
9. Remove side of the pan.
10. Prepare Royal Glaze by melting chocolate and cream in a small saucepan over low heat, stirring constantly until smooth. Do not boil. Let cool until thickened slightly (about 10-15 minutes).
11. Drizzle glaze over the cooled cake, allowing it to run down the sides. Use a narrow metal spatula to spread the glaze evenly on top and sides of the cake.
12. Allow the glaze to harden before serving. Garnish with pecan halves if desired. Serves 10-12.
